goals: Provide a command that executes `whereis python` and returns the result
Validate the NVST prototype creation flow with a straightforward implementation
Ensure the user can verify command execution and output capture

userStories.title: Execute whereis python command
userStories.description: As an end user, I want to execute the `whereis python` command so that I can locate the Python installation on the system.

acceptanceCriteria.text: The command executes successfully without errors
The output from `whereis python` is captured and displayed
The feature handles the command's return code properly

userStories.title: Verify command output
userStories.description: As an end user, I want to see the result of the `whereis python` command so that I can determine where Python is installed.

acceptanceCriteria.text: Output is displayed to the user in a clear format
The path(s) to Python installations are visible
If Python is not found, an appropriate message is shown

functionalRequirements.description: Execute the system command `whereis python` using Bun's process spawning (`Bun.spawn`)
Capture stdout and stderr from the command execution
Return the command output to the user or operator
Handle and report errors gracefully if the command fails
